rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks only into the target implementation scope when binding implementation-level outputs.
Preserve AADL component type structure by not introducing implementation-specific references into type-level annexes.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reference only features, constants, or equations visible at the target scope.
Implementation-level bindings (e.g., assignments) belong only in implementation annexes, not component type annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes use the syntax: annex agree {** ... **};
Use eq for derived expressions, const for named constants, and guarantee/assume for contract claims.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Avoid using subcomponent paths or implementation-private identifiers in component type annexes.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
